mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month increase in RSAFS signals expanding consumer demand that drives corporate revenue expectations higher, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of months when RSAFS rises over three consecutive months, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5 percent triggers restrictive financial conditions that raise discount rates and compress equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances over a 2-year walk-forward window, or the mean return conditional on FEDFUNDS > 3.5 is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly elevated CPI inflation above 300 and a federal funds rate above 3.0 percent create a stagflationary tightening bind where the Federal Reserve is forced to hike into a slowing economy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.1) during periods when both CPIAUCSL exceeds 300 and FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.0, or the signal frequency is below 5 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, an unemployment rate increase greater than 0.2 percentage points over three months triggers recession pricing as investors anticipate Fed overtightening and earnings downgrades, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of triggered instances during identified late-cycle periods, or the Rank IC is above zero in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in INDPRO exceeding 0.5 percent signals acute supply chain disruption and production shortfalls that immediately reduce expected industrial earnings, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of instances when INDPRO drops more than 0.5 percent month-over-month, or the conditional mean return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST exceeding 100 thousand units over three months signals collapsing residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the information coefficient of HOUST changes is above zero over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE confirm a tight labor market that supports wage income and household spending, lifting aggregate demand expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50 percent of joint-signal instances, or the combined Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarterly increases in GDPC1 signal a sustained demand expansion that validates earnings growth trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of instances following two consecutive quarterly GDP increases, or the Rank IC of GDPC1 changes is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
